justification: All pass criteria met: (1) Timeout mismatch clearly identified with exact math (200+150+800=1150ms vs 500ms inbound), (2) Orphaned downstream request consequence thoroughly described — upstream disconnects at 500ms while downstream calls continue up to 3000ms worst case, explicitly calls out 'wasted work' and 'dead requests', (3) Outbound>inbound relationship identified (each 1000ms outbound vs 500ms inbound, called out as '2x the budget EACH'), (4) Multiple remediation options provided: parallelization, timeout adjustment, caching, context cancellation, graceful degradation, (5) Verdict is NOT READY.

observations.text: Exceptional budget reality check box showing exact arithmetic: 1150ms sequential p99, 3000ms worst case, each outbound 2x the budget
Identified that InventoryService p99 alone (800ms) exceeds the inbound timeout — even parallelization doesn't fully solve it
Correctly noted absence of retries is actually appropriate given current timeout math
Flagged context cancellation as a blocker — upstream disconnect doesn't stop downstream work
Escalated the cross-team InventoryService SLA dependency and the product decision on partial enrichment
Health check coupling identified as an additional operational risk
Comprehensive observability gaps identified: no per-service latency histograms, no wasted-work metrics, no distributed tracing
Prioritized recommendations into blockers, week-1, and nice-to-have tiers
